key_drivers: Real yields falling sharply (-16bps 5-day, DFII10 at 1.97%) is the dominant bullish driver for equities, gold, and tech
Iran war creating largest oil supply disruption in history - bullish CL and gold; geopolitical risk premium elevated
VIX falling from elevated levels (23.87, -6.41% day, -26.61% 5-day) signals risk appetite recovery
Credit spreads narrowing (-25bps 5-day, HY OAS at 3.17%) supports equities especially small caps
BoJ hawkish tilt with 70% April hike probability is dominant JPY bullish driver; IMF urging continued hikes
EUR-USD rate differential (-150bps, ECB 2.00% vs Fed 3.50-3.75%) is dominant M6E bearish driver

stale_sources: Gold ETF flows (WGC requires login - inferred from gold at record $4,677/oz)
data_quality.notes: All FRED data fresh (Apr 2-3, 2026). Iran war context sourced from Reuters, Brookings, CFR (Apr 3-4, 2026). EIA inventory data for week ending Mar 27, 2026 (released Apr 1, 2026).
